# ArDrive Help Corpus

> Full public help content for AI agents and retrieval tools. Canonical URLs point to rendered help pages on ardrive.io.

## Index

- [ArDrive CLI](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-ardrive-platform-ardrive-cli/): Use the ArDrive CLI for terminal-based ArDrive workflows.
- [ArDrive Core](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-ardrive-platform-ardrive-core/): ArDrive Core contains the essential back end features for the ArDrive apps, written in TypeScript. While the Arweave network gives the power of.
- [Arweave File System](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-arweave-file-system/): Technical overview of ArFS, the Arweave File System used by ArDrive.
- [Drive Entity](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-drive-entity/): Technical overview of the ArFS Drive entity and root folder relationship.
- [Move File](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-move-file/): Technical overview of how ArFS represents moving files within a Drive.
- [Move Folder](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-move-folder/): Technical overview of how ArFS represents moving folders within a Drive.
- [Rename Drive](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-rename-drive/): Technical overview of how ArFS represents Drive renames.
- [Rename File](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-rename-file/): Technical overview of how ArFS represents file renames.
- [Rename Folder](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-rename-folder/): Technical overview of how ArFS represents folder renames.
- [Schema Diagrams](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-schema-diagrams/): Technical diagrams for public and private ArFS Drive structures.
- [Update File Version](https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-update-file-version/): Technical overview of how ArFS represents new file versions.
- [ArDrive On The Permaweb](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-on-the-permaweb-dapp/): Use the permanent ArDrive dApp entry point on the permaweb.
- [ArDrive Turbo](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-turbo/): Common questions about ArDrive Turbo Credits, payments, receipts, refunds, and support.
- [Arweave Wallets](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-arweave-wallets/): Common questions about Arweave wallets, seed phrases, and logging in to ArDrive.
- [Everything You Need To Know About ArDrive](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-everything-you-need-to-know-about-ardrive/): Common questions about permanence, privacy, uploads, sharing, and permaweb URLs in ArDrive.
- [How Do Keyfile And Seed Phrase Login Work](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-how-do-keyfile-and-seed-phrase-login-work/): Learn how keyfile and seed phrase login work and how to protect wallet access.
- [How Do Turbo Conversions Work](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-how-do-turbo-conversions-work/): Understand how Turbo upload prices are estimated from file size, AR, and Credits.
- [What Are The Privacy And Encryption Standards?](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-what-are-the-privacy-and-encryption-standards/): Private Drive files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM, while public files remain public forever.
- [What Currencies Or Cryptocurrencies Can I Use To Upload?](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-what-currencies-or-cryptocurrencies-can-i-use-to-upload/): ArDrive supports permanent storage payments through Turbo Credits or AR tokens.
- [What Makes ArDrive Different From Other File Storage Apps](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-what-makes-ardrive-different-from-other-file-storage-apps/): What makes ArDrive different from other file storage apps?
- [Where Can I Make A Feature Request](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-where-can-i-make-a-feature-request/): Where can I make a feature request?
- [ArDrive App Limits](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-ardrive-app-limits/): Current upload and download limits for ArDrive by upload method, platform, and browser.
- [Bad Gateway And Network Errors](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-bad-gateway-and-network-errors/): Troubleshoot 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, GraphQL, gateway, and frontend errors in ArDrive Web.
- [Cannot Access Or Retrieve A File](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-cannot-access-or-retrieve-file/): If a file will not load, preview, download, access, or retrieve from ArDrive Web and you see 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, or gateway errors, the issue may be temporary gateway availability.
- [Change Gateway URL For ArDrive](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-change-gateway-url-for-ardrive/): Advanced users can change the data gateway and GraphQL gateway that ArDrive uses from account settings or from the login screen.
- [Check Browser Console Logs](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-check-browser-console-logs/): Browser console logs and network errors are the best way to understand ArDrive Web issues and help support troubleshoot login, gateway, upload, download, preview, or sync problems.
- [Clearing Your Cache](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-clearing-your-cache/): Clear browser cache for app.ardrive.io when files, folders, drives, or statuses look stale.
- [Drive Syncing Issues](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-drive-syncing-issues/): Drive syncing issues are usually caused by intermittent gateway indexing delays and often resolve on their own within a few hours.
- [File Upload Issues And Workarounds](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-file-upload-issues-and-workarounds/): Troubleshoot file upload status, gateway indexing delays, and transaction visibility.
- [Login Trouble](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-login-trouble/): Troubleshoot ArDrive Web login issues, including password, keyfile, seed phrase, ArConnect, wallet, browser, and gateway-related login errors.
- [Sync Your Drive](https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-sync-your-drive/): Use regular sync or deep sync when files show red or orange status, recently uploaded files do not appear, or your drive looks stale.
- [Arweave Manifests](https://ardrive.io/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-arweave-manifests/): Learn how Arweave Manifests map transaction IDs to friendly file names and paths.
- [Block Explorers](https://ardrive.io/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-block-explorers/): Use Arweave block explorers to inspect transactions, wallets, and network activity.
- [What Is The Permaweb](https://ardrive.io/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-what-is-the-permaweb/): Learn what the permaweb is and how Arweave stores data permanently.
- [Actions](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-actions/): Overview of common file, folder, and Drive actions in ArDrive.
- [ArDrive Explorer View](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-ardrive-explorer-view/): An overview of the buttons on the ArDrive Drive Explorer:
- [Download A File](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-download-a-file/): Download files from ArDrive without additional storage charges.
- [Drive Explorer](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-explorer/): Once logged into ArDrive you’ll arrive at the Drive Explorer. Get to know how to use the New button to upload files and take actions to share your files.
- [Drive Sharing](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-sharing/): Share public or private Drives with a link.
- [Export Csv Table](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-export-csv-table/): Export a CSV inventory of Drive contents, transaction IDs, links, and metadata.
- [File Sharing](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-file-sharing/): Share public or private files from ArDrive with a link.
- [Hiding Files From Your Drive](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-hiding-files-from-your-drive/): Yes, hiding files or folders is possible with ArDrive.
- [Licensing Your Data](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-licensing-your-data/): Apply licensing information to uploaded files to clarify reuse and attribution.
- [Move A File Or Folder](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-move-a-file-or-folder/): Move files or folders within the same Drive.
- [New File Versions](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-new-file-versions/): Upload a replacement file with the same name to create a new file version.
- [Public Preview](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-public-preview/): Get a permanent public preview URL for files stored in a Public Drive.
- [Rename A File Or Folder](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-rename-a-file-or-folder/): Rename files and folders in ArDrive while preserving permanent data history.
- [Storing Your NFT Data With ArDrive Web](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-storing-your-nft-data-with-ardrive-web/): Store NFT assets and metadata permanently with ArDrive.
- [ArDrive Password](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-ardrive-password/): Understand how your ArDrive password protects private Drives and why it must be stored safely.
- [Create An Arweave Wallet With ArDrive](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-create-an-arweave-wallet-with-ardrive/): Using ArDrive requires an Arweave Wallet and creating a wallet is easy with ArDrive.
- [Fund Your Wallet](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-fund-your-wallet/): Fund ArDrive uploads with Turbo Credits or AR tokens.
- [Log Into ArDrive Keyfile](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-keyfile/): Log in to ArDrive using your Arweave wallet keyfile and password.
- [Log Into ArDrive MetaMask Wallet](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-metamask-wallet/): Log in to ArDrive with MetaMask and understand how the derived Arweave wallet is protected.
- [Log Into ArDrive Seed Phrase](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-seed-phrase/): Log in to ArDrive with an existing seed phrase and your ArDrive password.
- [Log Into ArDrive With Wander](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-arconnect/): Use Wander to manage an Arweave wallet and log in to ArDrive.
- [Attach A Drive](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-attach-a-drive/): Attach a shared public or private Drive to your account from a Drive link.
- [Create A Snapshot](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-a-snapshot/): Create Drive snapshots to speed up future syncing for large public or private Drives.
- [Create An Arweave Manifest](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-an-arweave-manifest/): Create an Arweave Manifest from a public Drive or folder to give permanent files friendly paths.
- [Create New Drive](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-new-drive/): Create a new public or private Drive for permanent file storage on the permaweb.
- [New Folders](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-new-folders/): Create folders to organize files inside an ArDrive Drive.
- [The New Button](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-the-new-button/): Use the +NEW button to create drives and folders, attach shared drives, upload files, and create manifests or snapshots.
- [Uploading Files](https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-uploading-files/): Upload files from a Drive using the +NEW button, review the one-time storage price, and wait for upload confirmation.

---

# ArDrive CLI

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-ardrive-platform-ardrive-cli/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: ArDrive Platform
Description: Use the ArDrive CLI for terminal-based ArDrive workflows.

# Introduction

The _[ArDrive Command Line Interface (CLI)](https://github.com/ardriveapp/ardrive-cli)_ is a Node.js application for terminal-based ArDrive workflows. It also offers utility operations for securely interacting with Arweave wallets and inspecting various [Arweave](https://ardrive.io/what-is-arweave/) blockchain conditions.

Data uploaded via the ArDrive CLI, once indexed by Arweave's Gateways and sufficiently seeded across enough nodes on the network, can be accessed via ArDrive Web.

All transactions successfully executed by ArDrive can always be inspected in the [Viewblock blockchain explorer](https://viewblock.io/arweave/).

# Intended Audience

This tool is intended for use by:

-   ArDrive power users with advanced workflows and resource efficiency in mind: bulk uploaders, those with larger storage demand, game developers, nft creators, storage/database admins, etc.

-   Automation tools or workflow platforms

-   Services or bots

-   Terminal aficionados

-   Extant and aspiring cypherpunks

For deeper integrations with the ArDrive platform, consider using the [ArDrive Core](/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-ardrive-platform-ardrive-core) (Node) library's configurable and intuitive class interfaces directly within your application.

# Learn More

Visit our docs portal to learn more - [https://docs.ardrive.io](https://docs.ardrive.io)

# Need Help?

Not sure how to start with the CLI or running into issues? Visit our [contact page](/contact) and we would love to help.

---

# ArDrive Core

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-ardrive-platform-ardrive-core/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: ArDrive Platform
Description: ArDrive Core contains the essential back end features for the ArDrive apps, written in TypeScript. While the Arweave network gives the power of.

# What is ArDrive Core?

ArDrive Core contains the essential back end features for the ArDrive apps, written in TypeScript. While the Arweave network gives the power of immutable data storage, the ArDrive Core can take your integration to the next level.

Using the ArDrive Core can unlock different capabilities for your app integration.

-   An append only file system schema called Arweave File System supporting metadata and version history

-   Battle tested patterns and components for querying, uploading and downloading

-   Privacy using industry leading encryption

-   View, organize and share all of your data through our cross-platform web app.

-   Cross-platform desktop apps to sync your data locally

-   Benefit from any ArDrive app upgrades as they appear

# Github

ArDrive-Core-JS can be cloned from our [Github](https://github.com/ardriveapp/ardrive-core-js), or deployed via NPM/Yarn.

Learn more on our docs portal at [https://docs.ardrive.io](https://docs.ardrive.io)

---

# Arweave File System

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-arweave-file-system/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of ArFS, the Arweave File System used by ArDrive.

Arweave File System, or “ArFS” is a data modeling, storage, and retrieval protocol designed to emulate common file system operations and to provide aspects of mutability to your data hierarchy on [Arweave](https://ardrive.io/what-is-arweave/)'s otherwise permanent, immutable data storage blockweave.

Due to Arweave's permanent, immutable and public nature traditional file system operations such as permissions, file/folder renaming and moving, and file updates cannot be done by simply updating the on-chain data model.

ArFS works around this by implementing a privacy and encryption pattern and defining an append-only transaction data model using tags within [Arweave Transaction headers.](https://docs.arweave.org/developers/server/http-api#transaction-format)

# Document Version History

**Version**

**Date**

**Release Notes**

0.11a

July, 2021

Introduces version 0.11, the first version of “ArFS” that has been in use in ArDrive since September 2020.

0.11b

October, 2021

Added more details around first ArFS version including actions that can be performed.

# ArFS Version History

**Version**

**Date**

**Release Notes**

0.10

August 2020

The brief, beta version that was in use during initial testing of ArDrive across Web (Dart) and legacy CLI (TypeScript).

0.11

(Current)

September 2020

Includes all of the major functionality supporting file systems on Arweave including new drives, folders, files, renames, moves and privacy.

Learn more about ArFS on in our official docs at [https://docs.ardrive.io](https://docs.ardrive.io)

---

# Drive Entity

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-drive-entity/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of the ArFS Drive entity and root folder relationship.

A Drive is the highest level logical grouping of folders and files.

All folders and files must be part of a drive, and reference the Drive ID.

When creating a Drive, a corresponding folder must be created as well. This folder will act as the Drive Root Folder. This separation of drive and folder entity enables features such as folder view queries, renaming and linking.

```
ArFS: "0.11"
Cipher?: "AES256-GCM"
Cipher-IV?: "<12 byte initialization vector as Base64>"
Content-Type: "<application/json | application/octet-stream>"
Drive-Id: "<uuid>"
Drive-Privacy: "<public | private>"
Drive-Auth-Mode?: "password"
Entity-Type: "drive"
Unix-Time: "<seconds since unix epoch>"

Data JSON {
    "name": "<user defined drive name>",
    "rootFolderId": "<uuid of the drive root folder>"
}
```

_Drive Entity Transaction example_

---

# Move File

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-move-file/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of how ArFS represents moving files within a Drive.

Files can be moved from one folder to another within the same Drive.

-   A new file metadata transaction is created when a user wants to move a file into a different folder.

-   The new file metadata transaction copies all of the file’s old metadata values, but the file’s `Parent-Folder-Id` must be updated to the `Folder-Id` of the folder is was just moved to.

    -   For private files, a new `Cipher-IV` is generated for this new metadata transaction.

-   File’s must not be allowed to be moved into a folder if a file exists in that folder with the same file name.

---

# Move Folder

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-move-folder/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of how ArFS represents moving folders within a Drive.

Folders can be moved from one folder to another within the same Drive.

-   A new file metadata transaction is created when a user wants to move a folder into a different folder.

-   The new file metadata transaction reuses the existing folder’s `Folder-Id` and copies all of it’s old metadata values, but the file’s `Parent-Folder-Id` must be updated to the `Folder-Id` of the folder is was just moved to.

    -   For private folders, new ciphers are generated for this new metadata transaction.

-   Folder’s must not be allowed to be moved into a folder if another folder exists in that folder with the same folder name.

---

# Rename Drive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-rename-drive/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of how ArFS represents Drive renames.

Drives can be renamed from one name to another.

-   A new root folder metadata transaction is created when a user wants to rename an existing drive.

-   The new root folder metadata transaction reuses the existing drive’s `Drive-Id` and `Folder-Id` and copies all of it’s old metadata values, but the drive’s and root folder’s `name` field in its Data JSON must be updated to the new drive name.

    -   For private drives, new ciphers are generated for this new root folder metadata transaction.

-   The new root folder transaction must not have have any `Parent-Folder-Id` since it is a root folder.

---

# Rename File

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-rename-file/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of how ArFS represents file renames.

Files can be renamed from one name to another.

-   A new file metadata transaction is created when a user wants to rename an existing file.

-   The new file metadata transaction reuses the existing file’s `File-Id` and copies all of it’s old metadata values, but the file’s `name` field in its Data JSON must be updated to the new file name and extension.

    -   For private files, a new `Cipher-IV` is generated for this new metadata transaction.

-   File’s must not be allowed to be renamed to the name of another file with that same name in that same folder.

---

# Rename Folder

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-rename-folder/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of how ArFS represents folder renames.

Folders can be renamed from one name to another.

-   A new folder metadata transaction is created when a user wants to rename an existing folder.

-   The new folder metadata transaction reuses the existing folder’s `Folder-Id` and copies all of it’s old metadata values, but the folder’s `name` field in its Data JSON must be updated to the new folder name.

    -   For private folders, new ciphers are generated for this new metadata transaction.

-   Folders must not be allowed to be renamed to the name of another folder with that same name in that same folder.

---

# Schema Diagrams

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-schema-diagrams/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical diagrams for public and private ArFS Drive structures.

Sometimes, it is easier to understand how different data types come together to make a single information architecture, or schema. The below diagrams show examples of how Drives, Folders and Files all fit together and reference one another.

# ArFS 0.11 Public Drive View

# ArFS 0.11 Private Drive View

---

# Update File Version

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-update-file-version/
Category: ArDrive as a Platform
Section: Arweave File System
Description: Technical overview of how ArFS represents new file versions.

When a user adds a new file to a folder, and there is a file in that folder with the same name, then a new file version is created.

-   A new file version uses the same `File-Id` of the file with the matching name and same `Parent-Folder-Id`.

-   The file upload process is followed and new File Metadata and Data transactions are created.

-   However a new UUID is not generated and the same `File-Id` and associated metadata is used for this new version

-   The new File Metadata Transaction points to the new Data transaction.

    -   Since the `File-Id` remains the same, the File Keys for private files can decrypt all versions of that file.

    -   For private files, new `Cipher-IV`s are generated for this new metadata and data transaction

-   ArFS clients can now iterate through the state of this file, since it will have multiple versions using the same `File-Id`.

---

# ArDrive On The Permaweb

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-on-the-permaweb-dapp/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Use the permanent ArDrive dApp entry point on the permaweb.

ArDrive is born & bred on the Permaweb and the app exists in a fully decentralized way on Arweave.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="w9Yi-ara1o" />

You can find the latest permanent version of the ArDrive web dApp at [https://dapp.ardrive.io](https://dapp.ardrive.io).

For everyday use, we recommend starting from [https://app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io). Use the permaweb dApp entry point when you specifically want to access the permanent app deployment.

---

# ArDrive Turbo

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-turbo/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Common questions about ArDrive Turbo Credits, payments, receipts, refunds, and support.

## **Credits, Payments, and Receipts**

What is a Credit?

-   Credits represent a 1:1 value with the AR token and can be used to upload data to ArDrive.

Can I view my Credits balance on Viewblock or Wander?

-   Credits are specific to ArDrive and can only be viewed and used in ArDrive.

Which currencies can I pay in?

-   USD with additional currencies being added soon.

Can I get a copy of my receipt?

-   All receipts are provided by Stripe and are sent to those that provide their email during check out.

Can I pay with any credit or debit card?

-   We accept all major credit and debit cards. Purchase restrictions may be implemented by your card provider or country of origin.

Do the value of Credits ever change?

-   Credits have the same storage purchasing power as AR.

Do you keep my credit card info?

-   No. ArDrive keeps a record of your public wallet address, the amount purchased and used Credits when purchases occur. All credit card purchases are processed by Stripe, learn more about their policies [here.](https://stripe.com/privacy "https://stripe.com/privacy")

Can I use Credits elsewhere on the permaweb?

-   Credits can be used with any apps on Arweave that accept Turbo.

## **Refunds, Transfer, & Exchanges**

Can I get a refund for my Credit purchase?

-   All sales of Credits are final. When a Credits are purchased, ArDrive holds that balance of AR to cover the cost of data uploaded.

Do Credits expire?

-   Yes. Credits expire 12 months (1 year) after the date of purchase. See our [Terms of Service](/tos-and-privacy) for details.

Can I transfer Credits to another wallet?

-   No, Credits are non-transferable.

Can I exchange my Credits for AR?

-   No, ArDrive is not a crypto exchange and Credits cannot be returned or exchanged.

Do I get my Credits back if my Turbo upload fails?

-   You will not be charged for any failed uploads. If you believe you have been charged please [contact](/contact) the ArDrive team.

## **Building with Turbo**

Can I use Turbo in my own app?

-   Yes. Visit the [developer docs](https://docs.ar.io/build) for current ArDrive and Turbo integration guidance.

Is Turbo Available in ArDrive-CLI?

-   Yes. Use the current [developer docs](https://docs.ar.io/build) for CLI guidance.

## **Don't see your question here?**

-   Submit a [support ticket](/contact).

---

# Arweave Wallets

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-arweave-wallets/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Common questions about Arweave wallets, seed phrases, and logging in to ArDrive.

I didn't get my seed phrase from ArDrive. Can I still use it to log in?

-   If you have a seed phrase from another Arweave-compatible wallet, such as Wander, you can use it to log in to ArDrive.

Can I get a seed phrase for my existing wallet?

-   No. If you did not save your seed phrase when the wallet was created, it cannot be generated or recovered later.

What is a seed phrase?

-   A seed phrase is a set of random words generated when you create a wallet. You can use it to log in or as a backup to regain access to your account.

What do I do if I lose my seed phrase?

-   You can still log in if you have your keyfile. If you lose both your seed phrase and keyfile, your wallet cannot be recovered. Keep your seed phrase in a safe place.

How do I keep my seed phrase safe?

-   Write it down on paper and store it securely, or save it in a password manager.

Is logging in with a seed phrase safe?

-   Yes. Always verify you are on [ardrive.io](https://ardrive.io) or one of the [official ArDrive dapps](/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-on-the-permaweb-dapp) before entering your seed phrase so you do not share it with another site or app.

Why is my wallet generated every time I log in with a seed phrase?

-   Your seed phrase identifies and regenerates your wallet each time you log in to ArDrive. Your wallet is required to access your drives, upload and sign transactions, and check your balance.

What format does my seed phrase need to be in to log into ArDrive?

-   Enter the 12 words in the order they were given to you, separated by spaces.

Can I use my ArDrive-generated wallet in Wander?

-   Yes. A wallet generated by ArDrive works with other apps in the permaweb ecosystem, including Wander.

---

# Everything You Need To Know About ArDrive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-everything-you-need-to-know-about-ardrive/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Common questions about permanence, privacy, uploads, sharing, and permaweb URLs in ArDrive.

Where is the delete button?

**ArDrive deleted the delete button.** All data uploaded to ArDrive is permanent and indelible. It can’t be removed by you or by us or by any third party. Think twice before uploading a file you wouldn’t want your great-grandchildren to discover.

However, only files that you make public can be seen by others. All files that you keep private will remain private unless you share them with others.

# What type of things should I put on ArDrive?

ArDrive provides permanent data storage. It’s an ideal platform for storing photos, video, audio, Word documents, and other files you don’t want to disappear as technology changes. The permaweb changes the way humans keep digital data.

Many different verticals are already benefiting from permanent data storage.

-   Family history

-   Academic papers and research

-   Legal papers and cases

-   Architectural drawings

-   Digital art

-   Original music

-   Journalists and bloggers

-   Digital archives

# What type of files should I not put on ArDrive?

Anything you wouldn’t want to be remembered for. ArDrive is about preserving essential archives, indelible memories and the best of human culture, not the worst.

If you put a file onto the public portion of ArDrive, it will be public forever. Please do not upload:

-   Personal information that includes banking, social security numbers, phone numbers, passwords, etc.

-   Profoundly offensive or objectionable content

-   Copyrighted materials

-   Proprietary information

# What kinds of files do you accept?

We accept a wide variety of files including video (mp4, mov), audio (mp3, wav), photos (jpg, png, tiff), vector files (svg, eps, ai), documents (Word, PowerPoint, PDF) and more. You can even save some AR by uploading .zip files. If it’s currently on your computer, you can probably add it to ArDrive.

# How do I upload more than one file at a time?

With ArDrive it is easy to upload more than one file at a time.

After you hit the ‘Upload File(s)’ button in ArDrive a browser will appear that allows you to select which files to choose from. Simply choose the desired files and then upload them.

Upload limits depend on upload method, browser, and platform. See [ArDrive App Limits](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-ardrive-app-limits) for the current limits.

If you accidentally choose the same file twice, ArDrive will catch this and ask if you really want to upload the same file twice. You will then have the option of declining the option to upload or proceed with the transaction.

If you want to upload a folder from your computer to ArDrive, there are two options:

1.  Zip the folder and upload that to ArDrive.

2.  Use the 'Upload Folder' option from the NEW button.

READ: [https://ardrive.io/bundles/](https://ardrive.io/bundles/) WATCH: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EUAERbzmxaY](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EUAERbzmxaY)

#
How long does it take to upload?

Upload speeds are dependent on your Internet connection. Arweave block times are 2 minutes and ArDrive waits for 15 confirmations to consider data properly seeded on the Network.

Need to upload quick? Give Turbo a try.

#
How long does it take to download?

Download speeds are dependent on your Internet connection. Permaweb data is cached and indexed by gateways & made retrievable just like downloading from the Internet.

#
Are there limits to the amount of data I can upload in one transaction?

Limits depend on upload method, browser, and platform.

**See also: [ArDrive App Limits](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-ardrive-app-limits)**

#
Is there data size limits on accounts?

No, there are no data size limits on accounts.

Feel free to put as little or as much on as you like on the permaweb.  Whether you want to store 1 kb, 1 MB, 1 GB, 1TB or 1 PB – it is up to you!

#
Will accounts ever be made inactive due to non-use?

No.  You can upload a file onto the permaweb, and walk away from it for 1, 2, 5, 10, 50 years and it will still be there. You will never receive a message that your account is going to be closed due to inactive use or a missed payment.

Think of it as your safety deposit box of data that you only have to pay for once.  As long as you keep the key, you can always access it.

#
How do I share my public drive?

One of the great features of ArDrive is sharing Drives with others. [Learn about Drive sharing](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-sharing).

#
Are my public files given a URL?

Yes, all of your public files are accessible through the permaweb & it's unique transaction ID. Use the [preview button](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-public-preview) to get the link to your data.

The public preview button will load or stream any compatible data right into your browser. This includes music, movies and PDFs.

When you click the public preview button, a new tab in your browser will open with the chosen file as a web page. The web address for the file will be located in the web address box of your browser. It may look unusual, but it works as a permaweb URL.

[Learn more about public previews](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-public-preview).

#
Can I edit my files after they are uploaded?

Arweave is a permanent blockchain. Whatever you upload to Arweave becomes immutable & can’t be edited or removed by anybody. Thanks to the [Arweave File System](/help/ardrive-as-a-platform-arweave-file-system-arweave-file-system), you can edit the metadata (name & location of your files on ArDrive). ArDrive will allow you to save a revised copy of the same file, and you will be able to see the history of your changes. Remember, **every file you upload to Arweave is a unique transaction.**

---

# How Do Keyfile And Seed Phrase Login Work

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-how-do-keyfile-and-seed-phrase-login-work/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Learn how keyfile and seed phrase login work and how to protect wallet access.

**Keyfiles:**

A keyfile is a file that contains your Arweave wallet, a unique sequence of characters that is used to encrypt and decrypt your data. This keyfile is used to authenticate your identity when you log in to ArDrive’s applications and sign transactions.

The keyfile is stored on your device and an encrypted version is stored in ArDrive until you opt to forget wallet during login, it can be removed from your device at any time. You will be prompted to provide your keyfile and password when you log in to ArDrive. Your password is needed to encrypt your wallet when you first start a session and decrypt your wallet for future access. Once your keyfile and password are verified, you will have access to your account and the data that you have stored via ArDrive.

It is important to keep your keyfile secure, as anyone who has access to it can potentially access your account and the data stored within it. Additionally, your wallet can not be exported from the app, and cannot be recovered by ArDrive. Therefore, it is recommended to store your keyfile in a secure location and not share it with anyone else.

**Seed Phrases:**

A seed phrase is a sequence of words that serves as a cryptographic backup for your wallet. When you enter the seed phrase, ArDrive applies an algorithm to identify and generate your unique wallet (which you can download as a keyfile).

You will be prompted to provide your seed phrase and password when you log in to ArDrive. Your password is needed to encrypt your wallet when you first start a session and decrypt your wallet for future access.

Once your wallet is generated and your password is entered, you can then view your balances, interact with your drives, and make uploads.

It is important to keep your seed phrase secure, as anyone who has access to it can potentially access your account and the data stored within it. We recommend keeping your seed phrase in a password manager for extra protection.

---

# How Do Turbo Conversions Work

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-how-do-turbo-conversions-work/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Understand how Turbo upload prices are estimated from file size, AR, and Credits.

# Getting the price of an upload

File size is used to determine the base fee to upload data to Arweave. The price of the base fee is set by the Arweave protocol code. ArDrive values Credits at 1:1 with AR when uploading. Prices for uploading in Credits are cheaper than uploading in AR because, while a Credit and AR token have the same value, a community tip is added on to all uploads paid for in AR

Users can purchase credits through ArDrive, and then use them whenever they need to upload their files.

**Note:** All file sizes are represented using binary units of measurement (i.e. 1 mB = 1024 kB).

The price of an upload using Credits takes into account multiple factors like the file size, Arweave network data cost, and other fees required by the service.

# Is the price of data consistent?

Not exactly. The Arweave network protocol is always adjusting the data price to maintain sustainable, perpetual storage. The data price is lowered as the network increases its overall capacity.

This can result in a fluctuating data price. However, for most files these fluctuations would only increase or decrease costs by fractions of a cent.

---

# What Are The Privacy And Encryption Standards?

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-what-are-the-privacy-and-encryption-standards/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Private Drive files are encrypted with AES-256-GCM, while public files remain public forever.

Are my files encrypted?

If you opt to make your Drive private, all of your files will be encrypted. ArDrive uses [AES-256-GCM](https://www.cryptosys.net/pki/manpki/pki_aesgcmauthencryption.html), which we believe is the best encryption standard currently available. AES-256-GCM is the current standard for top-secret military information. [Learn more](https://adeya.ch/communication-security-what-military-grade-encryption-means/)

Additionally, every private file has its own ‘private key’ based on the key from the user’s Arweave wallet combined with their password.  This makes it only possible for the content to be accessed by the person with that ‘private key’ (you) and the people that they share it with.

#
Can I make private files public?

Private Drives can't be converted into Public Drives yet. But any _files_ in your Private Drive can be shared with anyone you want.

Learn how to [share files](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-file-sharing) and [share Drives](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-sharing) in ArDrive.

#
Can I make public files private?

No, Arweave is a public data protocol. Public files can never be made private. Once you have made a file public it will always be public. There is no way to undo this.

**NOTE**: Sharing a private file with someone gives access to that file forever.

#
Can my public files be searched on the internet?

Yes, your public files can be searched on the internet.

We urge you to carefully consider what you want to make public, because once it is uploaded to the permaweb it will be there forever.

#
Can my private documents stay private forever?

Yes, your private documents can stay private. The only way they can become accessible is if you move them to a public folder, where they become available to everyone, or if you share the file with someone else.

---

# What Currencies Or Cryptocurrencies Can I Use To Upload?

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-what-currencies-or-cryptocurrencies-can-i-use-to-upload/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: ArDrive supports permanent storage payments through Turbo Credits or AR tokens.

How do I pay to upload my data on ArDrive?

There are two ways to purchase permanent storage with ArDrive:

1. **ArDrive Turbo**: use a credit or debit card to buy Credits.

ArDrive Turbo is an upload service that makes it simple to pay for permanent storage without managing AR directly. In the app, use the Turbo top-up flow to buy Credits and then spend those Credits on uploads.

Questions about Turbo? Visit the [Turbo FAQ](/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-turbo).

2. **Arweave Tokens (AR)**: use AR from your Arweave wallet.

ArDrive is based on the [Arweave network](https://arweave.org/ "https://arweave.org/") and the $AR token is used to pay the global network of operators who secure the network & provide storage.

Obtaining $AR through exchanges can be a cumbersome process. Exchanges may not be registered in your country and signing up can require complying with Know-Your-Customer laws.

[Fund your wallet](/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-fund-your-wallet) if you plan to upload with AR.

#
How does pay-per file work?

Pricing is based on the size of each file. ArDrive checks the size of each file and offers a price for permanent storage before upload.

Many of the files you upload will cost only a tenth or hundredth of a cent. It is not until the size of the file gets to be 1 MB that it may cost a penny.

**NOTE:** This is the price, not for one month or one year, but for forever.

#
How much does it cost?

Rather than pay subscriptions month after month, with ArDrive you only pay once to store your data forever. Most files such as photos, PDFs and Word Docs may only cost a tenth or hundredth of a cent.

There are a few factors that cause fluctuations in the price, such as the price movement of the AR token but the [network adjusts storage costs dynamically](https://ardrive.io/how-much-does-it-cost-to-store-on-arweave/) to keep costs predictable.

[**See Price Calculator**](https://prices.ardrive.io)

#
Will I be told how much it costs before I upload it to the permaweb?

Yes. Since every transaction could be different, ArDrive calculates the cost of each transaction before uploading to the permaweb. You will be given a chance to accept or reject the cost of the transaction before uploading your data permanently.

You can also use our [**Price Calculator**](https://prices.ardrive.io) to estimate how much fiat, crypto, data size, or AR is required to upload to the permaweb using ArDrive.

#
Does the price ever fluctuate?

Yes. The price of ArDrive uploads can fluctuate based on the computing power available to the Arweave network at any one time. We anticipate that these fluctuations should even out as the Arweave network increases in scope. [**See permaweb growth chart**](https://viewblock.io/arweave/stat/cumulativeWeaveSizeHistory)

There are a few factors that cause fluctuations in the price, such as the price movement of the AR token, but the network adjusts storage costs dynamically to keep costs predictable.

So you can be confident that even as the price fluctuates that you are still paying roughly the same amount for permanent storage.

---

# What Makes ArDrive Different From Other File Storage Apps

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-faq-what-makes-ardrive-different-from-other-file-storage-apps/
Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: What makes ArDrive different from other file storage apps?

What makes ArDrive different from other file storage apps?

ArDrive is not like cloud storage apps. Many of the file sync features are the same, but what makes ArDrive different is the key innovation of data permanence in a pay-as-you-go format.

WATCH: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IIBTLabA78k](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IIBTLabA78k) READ: [https://ardrive.io/what-is-ardrive/](https://ardrive.io/what-is-ardrive/)

ArDrive, which is built on [Arweave’s technology](https://www.arweave.org/), offers you the ability to securely store your data for a lifetime and beyond, without any subscription fees, and often for pennies per file or less.

This combination of permanence, security and value over time cannot be matched by any other storage service including new blockchain file storage providers such as Filecoin.

This is a new type of relationship with data and storage.

Similar to riding a bicycle for the first time, this new type of relationship to data will take a little getting used to, but once you get comfortable with it we think you will love it!

#
What is the permaweb?

ArDrive is built upon and powered by the permaweb.

Launched in 2018, the Arweave permaweb is an indelible, tamper-proof version of the internet. Sam Williams, the founding engineer of the ppermaweb, describes it as “a collectively owned hard drive that never forgets, \[and\] allows us to remember and preserve valuable information, apps, and history indefinitely.”

The permaweb was developed by [Arweave](https://www.arweave.org/) with technology that is based on blockchains and distributed computing, the computer science innovation behind digital currencies such as Bitcoin.

[Learn more about Arweave](https://arwiki.wiki)

[**Watch a Video about Arweave from the Coin Bureau (20:51)**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jKFhHL1geVw)

#
How can it actually be permanent?

Three factors allow Arweave’s technology to be a permanent storage system:

1.  _**Indelible Code**_.  The Arweave protocol or the code is written in a way that does not allow anything to be deleted.

2.  _**A Decentralized Network.**_  Instead of one centralized server that keeps all your data, files are distributed and replicated over a network of thousands of different nodes. Although one node will not necessarily store all the data, the data will be spread out countless times over all the nodes in the network to provide redundancy and rapid file access. As founder Sam Williams of Arweave explains, the permaweb “spreads the data across tens of thousands of places in the world and then makes it available from those locations, like the web, except censorship-resistant and permanent.” Even if one part of the permaweb goes offline, the remaining network retains the memory.

3.  _**Storage Endowment**_.  Like everyone else we need to pay someone to store the data. So how can a one-time payment cover the cost of storage for a lifetime or more? In most areas of life we are used to prices going up. However, this is not the case with the price of storage for data. It is very much the opposite as over the past 50 years the average annual rate of decline to store a GB has been 30.57%.

    The decline of storage prices has allowed the Arweave team design an innovate payment method to incentive storage over extremely long periods of time. The one-time up-front fee goes into an endowment where the interest on the fees will accrual perpetually and cover the cost of storage over time. In other words, the amount that a user pays upfront creates enough interest over time to cover the costs associated with paying someone to store that data perpetually.

    Just like a university or non-profit forms an endowment that creates interest to cover costs over time, the Arweave token creates interest that covers costs. Unlike those other endowments, the interest is covering costs that are declining and not rising.

    This design, in the end, creates a reward for the network (the people storing that data) that is greater than the cost to hold the data and maintain it—even over long periods of time. Even in the unlikely case that storage costs do not go down, the upfront free will cover 200 years of storage.

Want to learn more?  Read our post [**“Can data really be stored forever?”**](https://ardrive.io/can-data-really-be-stored-forever/)

#
What makes it censorship-resistant?

Whatever you put on the permaweb cannot be deleted by one single person or organization.

The permaweb’s file storage method was created so that each data storage block cannot be removed by anyone – not you, not ArDrive, not Arweave (the creators of the permaweb), not the world’s best supercomputer.

Furthermore, the Permaweb exists as a decentralized network. It distributes data to thousands of nodes which make it impossible for any centralized actor to remove the data from the whole network. A node may go offline, but the redundancy of the network keeps the entire repository of uploaded information available.

Therefore, with no delete button and a decentralized network, any public information posted on ArDrive cannot be taken down by a third party. This makes it censorship-resistant.

_In November 2020, a government made a failed attempt to censor the Permaweb._  [**Read the story in Fortune Magazine**](https://fortune.com/2020/11/05/chinese-spies-hack-anticensorship-startup/)

#
Where does my data go?

The data is not stored in any one single place. Instead, it is distributed across hundreds of different nodes throughout the world.

Your private information is encrypted end to end, and can only be unlocked by you and those you share the keys with.

#
What is the uptime or reliability of ArDrive?

The ArDrive Web App has had 100% uptime since launching our Beta app.

We cannot guarantee this perfect track record, but even if the ArDrive Web App is inaccessible or has an outage your data is still backed up and available from other nodes on the Arweave network. Cloud storage providers occasionally go offline, but the permaweb is more like the internet:  it can’t be turned off unless you power down every server globally.

Even if we completely go out of business, your files will remain on the permaweb. Your data is safe for generations to come. We are working on ensuring our web app is also published on the permaweb, ensuring you can still upload and download for the lifetime of your data.

* * *

**SUPPORT**
Have a specific question? Reach out via our [contact page](/contact).

**TRY ARDRIVE**
ArDrive offers never-ending storage of your most valuable files. Sometimes the best way to learn is to [try it out](https://app.ardrive.io/).

**COMMUNITY**
ArDrive is community-owned and operated. [Get in touch](/contact) or join us on [Telegram](https://t.me/ardriveapp) (general chat).

---

Category: FAQs
Section: FAQ
Description: Where can I make a feature request?

# Where can I make a feature request?

We’d love to hear what you have in mind. Please [reach out to us on Twitter](https://x.com/ardriveapp) with your idea or visit our [contact page](/contact).
