# ArDrive App Limits

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-ardrive-app-limits/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Current upload and download limits for ArDrive by upload method, platform, and browser.

Uploads are currently limited depending on how you are using ArDrive or which version of ArDrive you are using. We are always working to raise these limits and will update this page accordingly.

# Upload Size Limits

**Direct-to-network** uploads are paid with AR tokens, and are submitted directly to the Arweave network via an ar.io gateway.

**Turbo** uploads are paid with Credits, and are submitted to the ArDrive Turbo service.

The limits posted below are for ArDrive version **2.22.0** and above and apply to both public and private files.

| Platform | Direct-to-Network (AR) | Turbo (Credits) |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Web App | 2 GiB* | 2 GiB |
| CLI | 2 GiB | 2 GiB |

\* Direct-to-network uploads have no theoretical protocol limit; practical app and browser limits are listed here.

| Browser | Direct-to-Network (AR) | Turbo (Credits) |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| Chrome/Edge/Brave | 2 GiB* | 2 GiB |
| Safari | 2 GiB* | 500 MiB |
| Firefox | 2 GiB* | 500 MiB |

# Upload Count Limits

The ArDrive Web App has limitations on the amount of files it can upload.

Our testing has shown that Chrome/Brave Browsers can handle up to 1,500 files per upload & Firefox can handle up to 10,000 files.

# Download Size Limits

| Platform | Size |
| --- | ---: |
| Web App | 15 GiB* |

\* Downloads have no theoretical limits, however, we have tested files up to the listed size.

| Browser | Size |
| --- | ---: |
| Chrome/Edge/Brave | 15 GiB* |
| Safari | 1 GiB |
| Firefox | 15 GiB* |

# What is a GiB?

A gibibyte and a gigabyte are sometimes used synonymously, though technically they are not the same amount of storage. They are very close in size, however.

**One gibibyte equals 1.074 gigabytes**.

One gibibyte is equal to 2^30 or 1,073,741,824 bytes.

One gigabyte is equal to 10^9 or 1,000,000,000 bytes.

---

# Bad Gateway And Network Errors

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-bad-gateway-and-network-errors/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Troubleshoot 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, GraphQL, gateway, and frontend errors in ArDrive Web.

If ArDrive Web shows 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, GraphQL, gateway, or frontend errors, the issue is often temporary gateway availability or connectivity rather than a problem with your files or wallet.

ArDrive uses Arweave gateways to load account data, retrieve files, show previews, download files, and query drive, folder, and file metadata. During periods of heavy network activity, gateway maintenance, AR.IO routing issues, or temporary gateway errors, some requests may fail.

These errors can appear while logging in, syncing a drive, opening a folder, previewing a file, retrieving a file, or downloading a file.

The browser console is the best place to confirm what kind of error is happening and helps the ArDrive team troubleshoot if you need support. See [Check Browser Console Logs](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-check-browser-console-logs).

Common signs include:

- 502, 503, or 504 errors

- "Bad Gateway" errors

- Failed network requests while loading account, drive, folder, or file data

- Network or resource errors in the browser console

- Gateway, AR.IO, GraphQL, or frontend errors

# What to do

- Refresh ArDrive and try again.

- Wait a few minutes and retry if you are seeing 502, 503, 504, or bad gateway errors.

- Try a different browser or private window to rule out cached frontend issues.

- If the issue is with a file, try opening the file, preview link, or transaction ID again later after gateway indexing catches up.

- If your drive looks stale after the gateway recovers, run a regular sync or deep sync. See [Sync Your Drive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-sync-your-drive).

- Advanced users can try changing the data gateway and GraphQL gateway. See [Change Gateway URL For ArDrive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-change-gateway-url-for-ardrive).

- If the problem continues, visit our [contact page](/contact) and include what you were trying to do plus any browser console errors you saw, such as 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network errors, resource errors, AR.IO errors, or GraphQL errors.

---

# Cannot Access Or Retrieve A File

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-cannot-access-or-retrieve-file/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: If a file will not load, preview, download, access, or retrieve from ArDrive Web and you see 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, or gateway errors, the issue may be temporary gateway availability.

If you cannot access, retrieve, preview, or download a file in ArDrive Web, the issue may be related to temporary gateway availability, indexing delays, or frontend connectivity.

ArDrive retrieves file data through Arweave gateways. During periods of heavy network activity, gateway maintenance, AR.IO routing issues, or temporary gateway errors, a file may fail to load even if it is still stored permanently on Arweave.

If you are seeing 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, GraphQL, gateway, or frontend errors, see [Bad Gateway And Network Errors](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-bad-gateway-and-network-errors).

The browser console is the best place to confirm whether the file issue is a gateway, network, resource, GraphQL, AR.IO, or frontend error. See [Check Browser Console Logs](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-check-browser-console-logs).

Common signs include:

- 502, 503, or 504 errors

- "Bad Gateway" errors

- Failed download, preview, or file retrieval requests

- Network or resource errors in the browser console

- Gateway, AR.IO, GraphQL, or frontend errors while opening a file

# What to do

- Refresh ArDrive and try opening or downloading the file again.

- Wait a few minutes and retry if you are seeing 502, 503, 504, or bad gateway errors.

- Try a different browser or private window to rule out cached frontend issues.

- If you have a public preview link or transaction ID, try opening it again later after gateway indexing catches up.

- If the file should be in your drive but does not appear, or its status is stuck red or orange, try manually syncing your drive. See [Sync Your Drive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-sync-your-drive).

- Advanced users can try changing the data gateway and GraphQL gateway. See [Change Gateway URL For ArDrive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-change-gateway-url-for-ardrive).

- If the problem continues, visit our [contact page](/contact) and include the file link or transaction ID if available, plus any browser console errors you saw.

---

# Change Gateway URL For ArDrive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-change-gateway-url-for-ardrive/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Advanced users can change the data gateway and GraphQL gateway that ArDrive uses from account settings or from the login screen.

[app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/) connects to Arweave gateways to upload data, download data, and query indexed drive, file, and folder information.

Changing your gateway can be useful if the default gateway is temporarily unavailable, your ISP blocks access to it, or you want to use another decentralized gateway for Permaweb services.

This setting is intended for advanced users. Changing gateways can affect loading, syncing, previews, and how quickly newly uploaded data appears in the app.

# Change gateways from account settings

After you are logged in, open your account menu and go to settings.

![ArDrive account menu with settings option](/help/help-gateway-settings-1.jpg)

From settings, open the gateway settings area.

![ArDrive settings screen showing gateway settings](/help/help-gateway-settings-2.jpg)

You can update both gateway fields:

- Data Gateway: used for retrieving data and generating preview links.

- GraphQL Gateway: used for querying indexed drive, folder, and file metadata.

![ArDrive gateway settings fields](/help/help-gateway-settings-3.jpg)

# Change gateways while logging in

You can also change gateway settings from the login screen before accessing your drive.

![ArDrive login gateway settings](/help/help-gateway-login-settings.jpg)

# When to try this

Changing gateways may help if you are an advanced user troubleshooting drive syncing, file previews, or temporary gateway availability issues.

If your issue appears to be an extended outage, visit our [contact page](/contact) before changing settings.

---

# Check Browser Console Logs

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-check-browser-console-logs/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Browser console logs and network errors are the best way to understand ArDrive Web issues and help support troubleshoot login, gateway, upload, download, preview, or sync problems.

Browser console logs are often the best way to understand what is happening when ArDrive Web does not load, log in, sync, preview, upload, download, access, or retrieve a file as expected.

Console logs can show whether the issue looks like a password or wallet problem, a frontend issue, a failed network request, a gateway issue, an AR.IO issue, or a 502, 503, 504, or bad gateway error.

# How to open the browser console

- In Chrome, Edge, Brave, or Firefox, right-click anywhere on the page and select **Inspect**, then open the **Console** tab.

- You can also press **F12** or **Ctrl+Shift+J** on Windows and Linux.

- On Mac, press **Cmd+Option+J** in Chrome, Edge, or Brave. In Firefox, press **Cmd+Option+K**.

- In Safari, enable the Develop menu first from Safari settings, then choose **Develop** > **Show JavaScript Console**.

# What to look for

- 502, 503, 504, or "Bad Gateway" errors

- Failed network requests

- Network or resource errors

- AR.IO, GraphQL, gateway, or frontend errors

- Invalid password, invalid keyfile, wallet, or authentication messages

# What to send support

If you contact us, include what you were trying to do and the console errors you saw. A screenshot of the console is helpful.

Do not share your password, seed phrase, private key, or wallet keyfile. If a screenshot includes sensitive information, hide it before sending.

---

# Clearing Your Cache

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-clearing-your-cache/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Clear browser cache for app.ardrive.io when files, folders, drives, or statuses look stale.

Clearing the cache in your web browser for [https://app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io) can help when you think things aren’t behaving as they should. This includes:

-   Incorrect file status

-   Missing files, folders or drives

Syncing a large Drive can take several minutes depending on how many items are in it.

# Clearing Your Cache

## Brave Browser

-   brave://settings in the search bar

-   Privacy and security in the left menu

-   Cookies and other site data selection

-   See all cookies and site data selection

-   [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/) in the search bar

-   click garbage can icon and confirm

-   reload [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/)

## Chrome Browser

-   chrome://settings in the search bar

-   Privacy and security in the left menu

-   Cookies and other site data selection

-   See all cookies and site data selection

-   [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/) in the search bar

-   click garbage can icon and confirm

-   reload [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/)

## Firefox Browser

-   In the Menu bar at the top of the screen, click Firefox and select Preferences.

-   Select the Privacy & Security panel.

-   In the **Cookies and Site Data** section, click **Clear Data…**.

-   Remove the check mark in front of _Cookies and Site Data_.

    -   For more information about managing site data, see [Manage local site storage settings](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/storage).

-   With _Cached Web Content_ check marked, click the **Clear** button.

-   Close the _about:preferences_ page. Any changes you've made will automatically be saved.

## Safari Browser

1.  While Safari is running, select the first option, Safari, from the top left of your menu bar.

2.  Select Preferences… or use the keyboard shortcut \[CMD\] + \[,\]

3.  A pop-up window will open. Select the Advanced tab.

4.  Click the checkbox at the bottom of this tab that reads Show Develop menu in menu bar. A new item will now appear in the menu bar called Develop.

5.  Whenever you want to clear your cache, click on Develop and select Empty Caches.

6.  Reload the page you’re on, and you’ll be good to go.

---

# Drive Syncing Issues

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-drive-syncing-issues/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Drive syncing issues are usually caused by intermittent gateway indexing delays and often resolve on their own within a few hours.

If your drive is taking longer than expected to sync, it is usually caused by intermittent gateway indexing delays.

ArDrive relies on Arweave gateways to index newly uploaded files, folders, and drive updates. During periods of heavy network activity or temporary gateway delays, your data may take longer to appear or sync across the app.

In most cases, this resolves on its own within a few hours.

# What to do

- Wait a few hours and check your drive again.

- If the drive still looks stale, run a regular sync. If that does not find the missing files or folders, run a deep sync. See [Sync Your Drive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-sync-your-drive).

- Refresh ArDrive or sign out and back in if the app still looks stale.

- Advanced users can try changing the data gateway and GraphQL gateway. See [Change Gateway URL For ArDrive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-change-gateway-url-for-ardrive).

- If the problem lasts longer than a few hours, visit our [contact page](/contact) for outage updates or service status.

---

# File Upload Issues And Workarounds

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-file-upload-issues-and-workarounds/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Troubleshoot file upload status, gateway indexing delays, and transaction visibility.

When you upload your files to ArDrive, they are sent directly from your device to the decentralized storage network called Arweave where they are stored on servers across the world.

This process is not perfect, and sometimes your files do not appear on the network.

Let’s demystify what this means and what you can do about it.

# What is supposed to happen

After a file is uploaded, the Arweave network cryptographically secures the file’s transaction and distributes it across the different servers (miners) that make up the network. The file ends up “mined” in a “block” and is permanently stored and freely accessed.

End-to-end, this can take upwards of 30 minutes.

-   On average, blocks are mined every 2 minutes, but can sometimes take up to 15 minutes.

-   The miners building and maintaining the Arweave blockchain may periodically reorganize the transactions from last few blocks on the chain into different blocks (a.k.a. “fork recovery”), potentially dropping your transaction from the network history. However, each additional block added after the block containing your transaction, or “confirmation”, makes it increasingly less likely that your transaction will be reorganized into another block or dropped from the chain. Therefore, ArDrive waits for 15 confirmations before considering your transaction sufficiently “confirmed”.

-   Arweave Gateways have to index the transaction into a GraphQL index so ArDrive can find this file in the future. For “bundled” transactions like those that ArDrive uses to get you the best possible storage pricing, Gateway “unbundling” must take place before your transaction can be indexed and searchable by ArDrive. This process can take anywhere from a few minutes to a few hours during times of heavy network usage.

After files are mined and indexed, they are accessible via ArDrive and any other Arweave block explorer, like Viewblock.

# Tracking Upload Status

ArDrive tries to track the status of each transaction in order to let the user know when their files are stored on the network.

-   Green status means the file was successfully replicated and mined on the network.

-   Orange status means the file upload transaction to the network has completed, but ArDrive is awaiting a sufficient number of confirmations and any potentially necessary unbundling to complete.

    -   It could take up to 30 minutes for this process to complete, so please be patient.

-   Red status means the app believes the file upload transaction may have failed.

    -   NOTE: If a file’s transaction is not mined or is dropped by the network, the AR payment made for this file is effectively “refunded” by the network. However, this is not the case for failed or omitted unbundling by the Gateway.

# Troubleshooting “Red” Status

While it does not happen often, uploaded file transactions may not be “mined” successfully during periods of high network congestion or dropped by the network during a fork recovery. This could result in a “Red” file status icon.

We recently began packing all ArDrive transactions into bundles to improve ArFS reliability and reduce overall transaction costs for users. One tradeoff of this approach is that bundled transactions have to be unbundled by the Arweave Gateway, and this can sometimes take more time than expected. Soon we'll be making updates to the web app that will allow for more "unbundling" time at the Arweave gateway so that ArDrive transactions don't appear as 'failed' for the period of time that they are yet to be unpacked.

If your file status is red, the transaction may have failed and your file may not be permanently stored on the Arweave network. Use `turbo-gateway.com/` plus your transaction id to see if your file made it to Arweave 5–15 minutes after you upload your data through the web. If your file fails to reach Arweave, the fees you paid for uploading this data are not spent, and the funds return to your ArDrive wallet.

If the transaction later appears on the gateway but the file is still missing from your drive or its status stays red or orange, try manually syncing your drive. Start with regular sync, then use deep sync if the file still does not appear. See [Sync Your Drive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-sync-your-drive).

Using an Incognito Tab or logging out of ArDrive will clear your local cache of data, including files that have not been mined. If you log out and back in, the files marked red will disappear.

# Using the Arweave Block Explorer

Advanced users can look up every transaction they have made with their ArDrive Wallet by navigating to the Arweave Block Explorer, hosted by Viewblock

[https://viewblock.io/arweave](https://viewblock.io/arweave)

In the Block Explorer, you can search for your ArDrive Wallet address. It will show you every transaction you have made, including your ArDrive transactions. This is another way to validate your transactions and their status on the Arweave network.

Is the symbol for an Arweave Token transaction. These transactions involve moving AR tokens from one wallet to another.

This is the symbol for an Arweave Data transaction. These transactions involve uploading data to Arweave, and will include every ArDrive drive, folder and file upload.

This is the symbol for an Arweave Bundled Data transaction. These transactions include multiple files packed into a single bundle and can also include the ArDrive Community Tip as well.

---

# Login Trouble

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-login-trouble/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Troubleshoot ArDrive Web login issues, including password, keyfile, seed phrase, ArConnect, wallet, browser, and gateway-related login errors.

If you are having trouble logging in to ArDrive Web, first check whether the issue looks like a password, keyfile, seed phrase, or wallet problem.

If ArDrive shows an invalid password or invalid keyfile message, double-check that you are using the correct wallet keyfile and password.

If you are using a seed phrase, make sure the phrase belongs to the wallet you expect to access. If you are using ArConnect or another wallet connection, make sure the wallet is unlocked and connected to the correct account.

If you do not see an invalid password or wallet message, and instead see repeated network, resource, 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, AR.IO, GraphQL, or frontend errors, the issue may not be your password or keyfile. See [Bad Gateway And Network Errors](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-bad-gateway-and-network-errors).

The browser console is the best place to confirm whether the login issue is a password, wallet, gateway, network, or frontend error. See [Check Browser Console Logs](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-check-browser-console-logs).

# What to do

- Confirm you are using the correct keyfile, seed phrase, wallet, and password.

- Try a different browser or private window to rule out cached frontend issues.

- Refresh ArDrive and try logging in again.

- If the browser console shows 502, 503, 504, bad gateway, network, resource, AR.IO, GraphQL, or frontend errors, see [Bad Gateway And Network Errors](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-bad-gateway-and-network-errors).

- If the problem continues, visit our [contact page](/contact) and include how you are logging in, whether you saw an invalid password message, and any browser console errors you saw.

---

# Sync Your Drive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-sync-your-drive/
Category: FAQs
Section: Help And Troubleshooting Guides
Description: Use regular sync or deep sync when files show red or orange status, recently uploaded files do not appear, or your drive looks stale.

If your drive looks stale, recently uploaded files do not appear, or files show red or orange status longer than expected,ArDrivemay need to sync your drive data from the Arweave gateway again.

Some syncing issues are caused by temporary gateway indexing delays and resolve on their own over time. Manual sync givesArDriveanother chance to query the gateway and update the local view of your drive.

# Start a manual sync

Open the sync menu inArDriveand choose the sync option that matches what you need.

![ArDrive sync menu showing regular sync and deep sync options](/help/sync-menu.jpg)

# Regular sync

Regular sync is incremental. It starts from the last block heightArDrivesynced for your drive and checks for newer drive, folder, and file updates.

Use regular sync when:

- Your drive mostly looks correct, but recent files or folders are missing.

- A file status has not updated after waiting for gateway indexing.

- You refreshed the app and wantArDriveto check for newer updates.

# Deep sync

Deep sync starts from block height 0 and rebuilds the drive index from the beginning.

Use deep sync when:

- Regular sync does not find the missing files or folders.

- Your drive looks incomplete or significantly out of date.

- You changed gateways and wantArDriveto rebuild its view of your drive.

- Support asks you to run a deep sync while troubleshooting.

Deep sync can take longer than regular sync, especially for large drives, because it checks your drive history from the beginning.

# When sync may not fix it

If the gateway has not indexed the file, folder, or upload transaction yet, sync may still not find it right away. Wait a few minutes to a few hours and try again.

If you continue seeing gateway, GraphQL, 502, 503, 504, or bad gateway errors, see [Bad Gateway And Network Errors](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-bad-gateway-and-network-errors).

Advanced users can also try changing the data gateway and GraphQL gateway. See [Change Gateway URL For ArDrive](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-change-gateway-url-for-ardrive).

---

# Arweave Manifests

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-arweave-manifests/
Category: Understanding The Permaweb
Section: General
Description: Learn how Arweave Manifests map transaction IDs to friendly file names and paths.

# Introduction

Arweave Manifests unlock new and unique experiences that can only be made possible on the Permaweb.

An Arweave Manifest is a mapping of Arweave Transaction IDs to friendly names. This map is put into a standardized JSON structure and permanently stored on Arweave. This allows users to create logical groupings of content. Arweave Gateways interpret this structure so users can reference individual transactions by their file name and/or path.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="AhxXThWMwbY" />

This makes it easier for web masters, NFT creators or any other user to reference their transactions by a single “master” manifest transaction and the individual file path and name. They allow you to use friendlier URLs like...

[https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/**0.png**](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/0.png)
[https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/**333.png**](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/333.png)

...rather than opaque Arweave transaction IDs that ordinarily reference your data:

[https://turbo-gateway.com/**ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0**](https://eev46qzbxsuwzsou7p4xu3trfpa63x6b3hpon5l46hnrisudhswq.turbo-gateway.com/ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0)
[https://turbo-gateway.com/**nHuFsR2Y-e\_qtnsmj0avn37CTWxlZ4zvG\_CKwtldK3I**](https://turbo-gateway.com/nHuFsR2Y-e_qtnsmj0avn37CTWxlZ4zvG_CKwtldK3I)

This standard was created by the Arweave Team. More details on its motivation, schema and path resolution details can be found on [Github](https://github.com/ArweaveTeam/arweave/wiki/Path-Manifests).

\-----

With ArDrive, any Drive or Folder can be turned into a Manifest. The file names and paths are represented within the Arweave Manifest, making it easier for developers and users to reference their content by the names used on their local file systems.

**NOTE:** Arweave Manifests must be public, non-encrypted data for the Arweave Gateways to interpret them.

Want to learn more? Check out this deep dive into Arweave Manifests and demonstration on how to create them with ArDrive Web, CLI and Core-Js

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="KBP1J3hStDw" />

# \---

# Use Cases

Arweave Manifests tie many transaction IDs to one transaction ID. This opens a variety of use cases

-   Aggregate your NFT assets into a single Arweave transaction ID for easier Smart Contract publication.
-   Host a personal or business website

-   Host a web application using familiar frameworks like JavaScript, React and Angular

# NFT Publication

Digital creators, whether music, art or video (or anything in between) can leverage an Arweave Manifest in order to reference their permanently stored data within their NFT Smart Contracts using a predefined set of friendly names.

Here is an example manifest that groups 1,111 images that were part of an NFT drop. The artist did not want to sacrifice the naming scheme used to programmatically generate the artwork. The manifest references all 1,111 images with a single Arweave Transaction ID that can be used in an ERC721 contract.

The root manifest transaction (Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA) has a default `index` that corresponds to the Arweave Transaction ID for image number 0:

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/)

This image can also be reached using the manifest transaction plus its friendly name `0.png`:

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/**0.png**](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/0.png)

`0.png` could also be reached directly by its own unique Arweave Transaction ID: (ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0)

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0](https://turbo-gateway.com/ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0)

Any other file in the manifest can be seen by adding the corresponding filename, from `0.png` to `1110.png`:

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/**1.png**](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/1.png)

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/**333.png**](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/333.png)

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/**1110.png**](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/1110.png)

# Static Site Hosting

Static, HTML based web sites are another use for Arweave Manifests. Whether it is a custom developed site, leverages an easy to use template, or taken from an existing portal like WordPress, they can all be uploaded to ArDrive and tied together with an Arweave Manifest, and deployed as a website on the permaweb.

One example is the [ArDrive.io](http://ArDrive.io) Public Site, which is is hosted with Word Press.

The plugin [https://www.simplystatic.co/](https://www.simplystatic.co/) downloads all of the site assets;  pages, images, CSS etc.

The assets and their folder structures get uploaded to ArDrive and once the files are mined and validated, an Arweave Manifest can be created at the root of the Drive. The manifest ties all of the site’s files and their transactions into a single Arweave transaction that loads a permanent version of the website.

See ArDrive's website assets in a public Drive:

-   [https://app.ardrive.io/#/drives/318826ae-472b-46ea-96db-b6a397a201ac?name=ArDrive.io+Public+Site](https://app.ardrive.io/#/drives/318826ae-472b-46ea-96db-b6a397a201ac?name=ArDrive.io+Public+Site)

A manifest is made of that Drive (dKA3lLa10okiDj5w7s502YHOzc\_Pg-ZM3y8y0hQ8v8E) and is now a site on the permaweb.

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/dKA3lLa10okiDj5w7s502YHOzc\_Pg-ZM3y8y0hQ8v8E/](https://turbo-gateway.com/dKA3lLa10okiDj5w7s502YHOzc_Pg-ZM3y8y0hQ8v8E/)

**NOTE:** If you need to update your website, a new manifest is needed to capture changes to your website.

# React App Hosting

React apps also work with manifests and can be permanently served from the Permaweb.

The ArDrive Price Calculator [https://prices.ardrive.io](https://prices.ardrive.io) is an example React app, that is hosted both centrally and on the decentralized PermaWeb.

The root manifest transaction has a default `index` that corresponds to `index.html` which has all of the references to the assets needed to load the ArDrive Price Calculator.

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/](https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/)

Assets can be loaded individually by adding their file name and path. For example

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/font.css](https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/font.css)

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/dist/index.js](https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/dist/index.js)

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/dist/utils/index.js](https://turbo-gateway.com/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo/dist/utils/index.js)

All of the React assets are hosted out of a public drive, in a single folder called `build`.

-   [https://app.ardrive.io/#/drives/cc5ad940-83ba-4cd4-9192-669867030904?name=price-calculator](https://app.ardrive.io/#/drives/cc5ad940-83ba-4cd4-9192-669867030904?name=price-calculator)

Need to change a file? Items in a manifest can be modified, like changing the name, uploading a new version or moving to a new folder. A new Arweave Manifest can be generated with the updated references.

Each version of the manifest is stored permanently, allowing web site and web app version control back to the first iteration ever published.

# Raw Endpoint

To view all the paths of a manifest, use the \`raw\` endpoint followed by the manifest transaction ID:

-   [https://turbo-gateway.com/raw/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo](https://turbo-gateway.com/raw/QMbW2XMKGvr7UgzV3tUxxnXTsCgRJRnCaEZC9Vxh9Yo)

# Ready to start?

ArDrive is an easy way to create a manifest out of a Drive or Folder from the NEW button. Check out how to [create an Arweave Manifest](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-an-arweave-manifest/) with ArDrive Web.

For a more advanced Manifest creation experience, like creating manifests out of Private ArDrive Data or totally customizing a manifest to use any Arweave Transaction ID or friendly name, visit our [docs portal](https://docs.ardrive.io/docs/cli/using-the-cli.html#uploading-manifests).

---

# Block Explorers

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-block-explorers/
Category: Understanding The Permaweb
Section: General
Description: Use Arweave block explorers to inspect transactions, wallets, and network activity.

There are two block explorers that can be used to view details about all of the transactions, wallets, and Smartweave contracts on Arweave.

-   [ViewBlock](https://viewblock.io/arweave)

-   [https://scar.vercel.app/#/](https://scar.vercel.app/#/)

For more info, check out this video about [how to use Viewblock.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SWyLTcCwcLI)

---

# What Is The Permaweb

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-what-is-the-permaweb/
Category: Understanding The Permaweb
Section: General
Description: Learn what the permaweb is and how Arweave stores data permanently.

Arweave stores data permanently and sustainably over time for a single up front fee. It is a data storage protocol as a service, where data is stored on an immutable blockchain and owned by a global community, [accessible through gateways](https://ar.io/what-is-a-gateway/) that make up [the permaweb](https://ar.io/what-is-the-permaweb/).

Similar to Bitcoin and Ethereum, Arweave is [free and open source software](https://github.com/ArweaveTeam/arweave). This software is run across thousands of nodes across the world. Each node is responsible for the security of the blockweave, Arweave’s version of a blockchain.

The blockweave was purpose built for the storage and replication of data over [standard HTTP and TCP/IP.](https://docs.arweave.org/developers/server/http-api) Data on the blockweave is fault tolerant, tamper proof, and paid for - Arweave is a new way to store data, unmatched by centralized storage platforms.

The native [Arweave token (AR)](https://messari.io/asset/arweave/profile) is used to pay for permanent storage space. The [price of data](https://prices.ardrive.io) is calculated by the network and each payment is split between node operators (also known as miners) and [the endowment](https://arwiki.wiki/#/en/storage-endowment). This endowment covers the cost of storing the data for at least 200 years, modeled after declining storage costs over time.

Arweave allows for a permanent and decentralized Internet. The Arweave Permaweb is a global web of pages, and applications that live forever on Arweave. Permaweb apps use a set of open, modular and interchangeable protocols designed and developed by the community.

---

# Actions

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-actions/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Overview of common file, folder, and Drive actions in ArDrive.

After your data is uploaded and secured on the Arweave network, ArDrive lets you perform various actions to edit, share and download your content.

**Drives:** When in a drive you can click on the “Show Menu” Button

This will bring up the dropdown:

You will now have your choice to Rename, Share, Export the Drive Contents or see the details (More info) of the Drive.

**Folder**: Clicking on a folder will bring up the details bar to the right and the ability to Rename or Move.

**Files**: Clicking on a file will bring up the details bar and the ability to:

-   Share the file

-   Download the file

-   Preview the file (will show you the permanent URL given to the file in your web browser)

    -   **Note:** Only for Public files and not Private Files

-   Rename the file

-   Move the file

For a more in-depth guide of all these functions see below:

-   [Download a file](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-download-a-file/)

-   [Share a file](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-file-sharing/) or [drive](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-sharing/)

-   [Preview](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-public-preview/) a public file

-   [Edit](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-rename-a-file-or-folder/) a file or folder name

-   [Move](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-move-a-file-or-folder/) a file or folder name

[See what all the buttons on the Drive Explorer do](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-ardrive-explorer-view/).

---

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-ardrive-explorer-view/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: An overview of the buttons on the ArDrive Drive Explorer:

An overview of the buttons on the ArDrive Drive Explorer:

# Download A File

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-download-a-file/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Download files from ArDrive without additional storage charges.

Click on the file you want to download.

In the details panel, the download icon is the one with the arrow pointing downwards.

Click on the download icon and the file will download to your computer.

For private files, we remove any ArFS encryption.

**NOTE**: There is no additional charge to download, preview or share any of your files in ArDrive.

---

# Drive Explorer

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-explorer/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Once logged into ArDrive you’ll arrive at the Drive Explorer. Get to know how to use the New button to upload files and take actions to share your files.

Once logged into ArDrive you’ll arrive at the Drive Explorer.

Get to know how to use [the New button](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-the-new-button) to upload files and [take actions](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-actions) to share your files.

---

# Drive Sharing

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-drive-sharing/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Share public or private Drives with a link.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="O3o0oLc2IX4" />

One of the great features of ArDrive is sharing Drives with others.

To share a Drive select the Public Drive you wish to share.

Then click on the show menu icon at the right of the Drive name

After clicking on the icon a text box will appear that has a link that you are able to copy and send to anyone of your choosing.

**When the user receives your link and clicks on it they will be brought to that specific Drive and will be able to access the shared drive without needing an ArDrive account or login.**

* * *

When you share a **Private Drive**, the "drive key" is included in the copied link.  This enables someone to view the Drive without having access to your wallet, or drive password.

**Make sure you know & trust who you’re sharing your Private Drive & Files with & where you’re sharing it.**
**It is not recommended to share Private Drives & Files on public platforms.**

If the recipient of this drive key loses it, they will no longer be able to access the private drive.

Sharing is only for Public Drives, Private Drives and individual files. Folders cannot currently be shared.

---

# Export Csv Table

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-export-csv-table/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Export a CSV inventory of Drive contents, transaction IDs, links, and metadata.

Exporting a CSV file of your Drives contents work with both public & private Drives.

The CSV contains the following information:

-   File ID

-   File Name

-   Parent Folder ID

-   Data Transaction ID

-   Metadata Transaction ID

-   File Size

-   Date Created

-   Last Modified

-   Direct Download Link from turbo-gateway.com

    -   Direct Download Links from a Private Drive are encrypted & won’t be accessible without the file key.

Use the [Share File](/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-file-sharing/) feature to get a link with the file key.

---

# File Sharing

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-file-sharing/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Share public or private files from ArDrive with a link.

To share a file in one of your public or private folders click on and highlight the file you want to share.

This will expand the detail panel. at the top right corner of your screen

### File Link

Click on the share icon and a window will pop up in the middle of your screen that contains a link. You can now copy the link and send it to whomever you want to share it with and they will have access to the file forever.

**NOTE:** Sharing is only for Public Drives, Private Drives, and individual files. Folders cannot be shared at this time.

**NOTE:** Whomever you share a link with can also share that link with others. We plan to provide the option to limit the spread of link sharing in future versions of ArDrive. For now, links are unrestricted in how often they can be shared.

---

# Hiding Files From Your Drive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-hiding-files-from-your-drive/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Yes, hiding files or folders is possible with ArDrive.

### **Can you hide a folder or file?**

Yes, hiding files or folders is possible with ArDrive.

**NOTE: Drives cannot be hidden at this time**

To hide a file or folder press the ‘three dots’ to the right of the file or folder, which will reveal the option to hide that particular file or folder.

Click on ‘Hide’ and that folder or file will be hidden from view.

### Can you unhide files or folders?

If you want to unhide the files or folders that you have hidden, go to the ‘three dots’ to the right of Drive box and hit the ‘Reveal hidden items’ option.

To hide these items again press the same ‘Three Dots’ and hit ‘Conceal hidden items’.

---

# Licensing Your Data

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-licensing-your-data/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Apply licensing information to uploaded files to clarify reuse and attribution.

# Introduction

ArDrive is proud to introduce its new "Licensing" feature, designed to empower users with greater control and flexibility over the distribution and use of their uploaded files. This guide provides an in-depth overview of the licensing options available, including the Universal Data License (UDL) and a suite of Creative Commons licenses. By understanding and utilizing these licensing options, you can ensure that your work is shared and used in a manner that aligns with your preferences.

# Licensing Your Content

When you upload files to ArDrive, you now have the option to apply a license after. This feature is vital for content creators, educators, and anyone interested in specifying how their work can be used by others. Licensing your content can protect your rights while also enabling you to contribute to the global commons of knowledge and culture.

## How to Apply a License

1.  **Upload Your File:** Begin by uploading your file(s) to ArDrive as you normally would.
2.  **Assign License:** After the upload process, select the file and apply a license.
3.  **Select a License:** You can select from the Universal Data License (UDL) or any of the Creative Commons licenses.
4.  **Review and Confirm:** After selecting a license, review the details to ensure it aligns with your intentions for the file. Once confirmed, the license will be applied to your file.

At this time, there is no additional cost to apply a licenses files on ArDrive.

## Viewing Licenses

Anyone accessing your files on ArDrive can view the license you've applied by viewing the file's details in the right panel. This transparency ensures that users understand the permissions and restrictions associated with a file, whether accessing an individual file or the full drive.

# Understanding Your Licensing Options

## Creative Commons Licenses

Creative Commons is a globally recognized nonprofit organization that offers a standardized way to grant public permission to share and use creative work. By applying a Creative Commons license to your files, you specify the freedoms and limitations others have in using your work. This clarity benefits both creators and users, facilitating the sharing and use of copyrighted material in a legal and straightforward manner.

### The Six License Types

Creative Commons offers six main license types, ranging from the most permissive (allowing for both commercial use and modifications) to the most restrictive (only allowing others to download your works and share them with others as long as they credit you, but they can’t change them in any way or use them commercially). Additionally, the CC0 Public Domain Dedication allows creators to waive all rights and place a work in the public domain.

Learn more from [Creative Commons](https://creativecommons.org/share-your-work/cclicenses/) about these licenses, as well as CC0 Public Domain Dedication.

### Choosing the Right License

Selecting the appropriate license is crucial. We recommend using the [Creative Commons License Chooser](https://chooser-beta.creativecommons.org/) tool, which guides you through the process of selecting a license that aligns with your intentions.

## Universal Data License (UDL)

The Universal Data License is specifically designed for content stored on public permissionless blockchain networks. It is a legal framework that outlines the terms of use for your content, addressing the unique considerations of blockchain-based storage. The UDL is an excellent choice for users who wish to leverage the security and transparency of blockchain technology for their content.

[Learn more](https://dev.to/fllstck/the-universal-data-license-explained-2di) about the Universal Data License and how it works.

## Impacts of Licensing

Applying a license to your content has several implications:

-   **Legal Clarity:** Licenses provide legal clarity about what others can and cannot do with your content, protecting your rights while facilitating its use.
-   **Control:** You maintain control over how your content is used and distributed.
-   **Contribution to the Commons:** By selecting a license that allows for sharing and reuse, you contribute to the global commons, supporting a culture of collaboration and innovation.

# Stay Connected and Empowered

If you have any questions, need assistance with the licensing feature, or require further information, please don't hesitate to reach out. Our support team is here to help you with technical queries, licensing choices, and any other concerns you might have.

Visit our [contact page](/contact) for help with technical queries, licensing choices, and any other questions.

---

# Move A File Or Folder

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-move-a-file-or-folder/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Move files or folders within the same Drive.

To move a file or folder to another folder, first click on the file or folder you want moved.

In the details panel, the move button is on the right end.

This will bring up a window titled “**Move File**” which has all of the Folders you have created within that Drive. Select the folder or subfolder where you’d like to move the file, then click the “**Move Here**” button at the bottom right.

**NOTE**: Files and folders cannot currently be moved from one Drive to another.

---

# New File Versions

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-new-file-versions/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Upload a replacement file with the same name to create a new file version.

Uploading a new file version is a matter of uploading a file with the same name as an existing file in a given folder. ArDrive will automatically create a second version of that file, using the fieId as the unique ID used to access the entire version history of the file.

There are no version limits, and each new version contains the full data of that file.

Learn more about the [Arweave File System](http://ardrive.io/arfs).

Note: ArDrive exposes version history in the Info panel of the file.

---

# Public Preview

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-public-preview/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Get a permanent public preview URL for files stored in a Public Drive.

All of the files on your Public Drive are given a URL that can be searched and shared as any other web address.

To locate the full URL address highlight a file within your public folder.

In the details panel, select the square with a diagonal arrow - this is the public preview button. It will load any browser compatible data. This includes photos, music, movies, and PDFs.

## URLs on the Permaweb

When you click the public preview button, a new tab in your browser will open. You're viewing your data on the permaweb. The first string of numbers is a sandbox that turbo-gateway.com uses, the string of characters after turbo-gateway.com is the data item's transaction ID.

Once you have this unique URL you can share it with others, embed it on a website or bookmark it in your browser as a record of your public permaweb files.

**Warning:**  Not all file types can be previewed in the browser. If a file type cannot be previewed, it will automatically download the file to your computer.

---

# Rename A File Or Folder

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-rename-a-file-or-folder/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Rename files and folders in ArDrive while preserving permanent data history.

To rename a file or folder, click on the file or folder you want to be renamed.

In the details panel, click on the icon that looks like a pencil.

Clicking the icon will bring up a “Rename File” window with the current name of the File displayed in the text box.

Click the “Rename” button below and the file get its new name. A new transaction will be submitted to the Arweave network, using this new name with the file’s existing data.

**NOTE:** All data stored in ArDrive is immutable and cannot be changed. But this doesn’t mean we cannot append new information and metadata to that file.

ArDrive lets you do things like rename and move your files and folders after they have been uploaded. This ability to edit the metadata lets you keep a nice organized view of your permanent data.

Learn more about [ArFS](https://ardrive.io/arfs) in our FAQ Video: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9gv4S4K4Kro](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9gv4S4K4Kro)

---

# Storing Your NFT Data With ArDrive Web

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-actions-storing-your-nft-data-with-ardrive-web/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Actions
Description: Store NFT assets and metadata permanently with ArDrive.

Whether you are creating NFTs on Ethereum, Solana, or Tezos, storing your NFT data and metadata with ArDrive means you do not have to worry about it disappearing.

ArDrive makes your art & metadata fault tolerant and tamper proof.

Storing your NFT data on ArDrive is a simple and straightforward process.

_At this point, we’re assuming that you have already created an account and are logged in to ArDrive. If not, get started with our_ [_Create a Wallet_](/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-create-an-arweave-wallet-with-ardrive) _article first._

## **Create a Public Drive**

You want your NFT to be seen - it needs be in a public Drive!

## **Upload your image files**

## **Upload your NFT metadata**

Our good friend Cowboy Coder went in depth into [NFT metadata](https://ardrive.io/arweave-and-nft-metadata/). Upload your metadata the same way you uploaded your images.

## **Create a Manifest for your metadata**

Just like you made one for your images.

## **Working with NFT marketplaces**

Now you have an NFT on the Arweave blockweave where both the transaction and image will last permanently.

You can now upload your NFT to other popular NFT marketplaces like [OpenSea](https://opensea.io/) with confidence that the NFT that you are selling will be around forever!

When listing your NFT be sure that you share your Arweave link with your file’s Data Transaction ID, eg `https://turbo-gateway.com/4OIZ1pAi3KZvEnZZ1af15BIuL3Y_ql32WWIrTpQc69Y`

This is sometimes referenced as

`ar://4OIZ1pAi3KZvEnZZ1af15BIuL3Y_ql32WWIrTpQc69Y`

---

# ArDrive Password

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-ardrive-password/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Understand how your ArDrive password protects private Drives and why it must be stored safely.

## ArDrive requires a password for any Private Drives that you create.

##

Your ArDrive password serves as additional security during your session. Encrypting your wallet at rest and as also serving as entropy for encrypting Private Drives.  This password is never uploaded or stored off your device, ensuring nobody else can access your data, except for you and those who you share it with.

**This password cannot be reset or changed if you make a Private Drive during your session. Make sure to write it down & keep it in a secure location since there is no recovery mechanism.  If this password is lost, you will not be able to decrypt your Private Drive data!**

Accounts that don't create private Drives can use different passwords.

---

# Create An Arweave Wallet With ArDrive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-create-an-arweave-wallet-with-ardrive/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Using ArDrive requires an Arweave Wallet and creating a wallet is easy with ArDrive.

## Using ArDrive requires an Arweave Wallet and creating a wallet is easy with ArDrive.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="kUaCpRXItss" />

1.  Visit [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io) and click **“New User? Get Started here”.**

2.  We will walk you through a few things that may be new to you compared to other apps in the signup process.

3.  A 12 word seed phrase and keyfile are generated with the option to download the keyfile.

4.  Write down the seed phrase & store it in a secure location.

5.  Confirm the seed phrase by providing the words ArDrive asks for in order to download the keyfile.

6.  Download your keyfile and keep it in a safe place.

7.  Create and confirm a password that you will use with your seed phrase or keyfile if you create a private Drive.

    **NOTE:** The seed phrase cannot be recovered by anybody. The keyfile can be recovered by using the seed phrase in ArDrive or another wallet service like Wander.

---

# Fund Your Wallet

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-fund-your-wallet/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Fund ArDrive uploads with Turbo Credits or AR tokens.

There are two ways to purchase permanent storage with ArDrive:

1. **ArDrive Turbo**: use a credit or debit card to buy Credits.

ArDrive Turbo is an upload service that makes it simple to pay for permanent storage without managing AR directly. In the app, use the Turbo top-up flow to add Credits to your wallet.

Questions about Turbo? Visit the [Turbo FAQ](/help/faqs-faq-ardrive-turbo).

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="H3GuC033w5o" />

2. **Arweave Tokens (AR)**: use AR from your Arweave wallet.

ArDrive is based on the [Arweave network](https://arweave.org/ "https://arweave.org/") and the $AR token is used to pay the global network of operators who secure the network & provide storage.

If you choose to use AR, buy it through an exchange or swap service that is available in your region, then transfer the AR to your Arweave wallet address. Always verify wallet addresses carefully before sending funds.

---

# Log Into ArDrive Keyfile

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-keyfile/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Log in to ArDrive using your Arweave wallet keyfile and password.

### Logging into ArDrive with your keyfile is an easy way to sign into ArDrive web.

1.  Go to [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/ "https://app.ardrive.io") and choose ‘Select a Keyfile’ to proceed.

2.  Locate the keyfile on your computer and select it. [ArDrive encrypts the keyfile securely on the backend](/help/faqs-faq-how-do-keyfile-and-seed-phrase-login-work "/help/faqs-faq-how-do-keyfile-and-seed-phrase-login-work").

3.  Enter your ArDrive password to log in.

Learn more about keyfiles in the FAQ Section.

---

# Log Into ArDrive MetaMask Wallet

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-metamask-wallet/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Log in to ArDrive with MetaMask and understand how the derived Arweave wallet is protected.

Users can login to ArDrive with MetaMask, making it easy to get started with storing data permanently without needing a new wallet browser extension or keys to worry about.

From [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io), select Log In, and Continue with MetaMask.

This will begin a process that generates a new Arweave wallet behind the scenes, that is safely and securely derived from your Ethereum wallet in MetaMask.

You'll be prompted to connect the wallet to ArDrive and sign two messages through MetaMask.

If it's the first time you're logging into ArDrive with MetaMask, you'll be prompted to create a password. **It's important to securely store this password, as entering a different password will prevent access to the newly created Arweave wallet.  It cannot be changed or recovered by an administrator.**

Users continue through the ArDrive onboarding steps and enter the app securely logged in with a new Arweave wallet derived from Ethereum keys through MetaMask. Users can write down or copy the seedphrase for the new Arweave wallet or download the keyfile. Users can skip this and continue to access ArDrive through MetaMask with the password created during setup.

Once logged in, users can top up using their credit card with Turbo, or even send and spend AR from their new Arweave wallet.

Want to export your Arweave key and store it in an Arweave wallet? Keyfiles can always be downloaded after logging into ArDrive by clicking your wallet address in the top right corner.  These can be stored securely offline, or within Arweave wallet apps like Wander.io.

---

# Log Into ArDrive Seed Phrase

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-seed-phrase/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Log in to ArDrive with an existing seed phrase and your ArDrive password.

### Logging into ArDrive with your seed phrase is a secure way to log into ArDrive without having to keep a keyfile on your computer.

1.  Go to [app.ardrive.io](https://app.ardrive.io/ "https://app.ardrive.io") and select ‘Enter Seed Phrase’ to proceed.

2.  Enter the 12 word seed phrase in the order it was given seperated by a space. When entered correctly, ArDrive generates the keyfile securely on the backend keeping it encrypted within the app.

3.   Enter your ArDrive password to log in.

Learn more about Seed Phrases in the FAQ Section.

---

# Log Into ArDrive With Wander

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-getting-started-log-into-ardrive-arconnect/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Getting Started
Description: Use Wander to manage an Arweave wallet and log in to ArDrive.

What is Wander?

Wander is a Chrome browser extension that makes it easy to manage your digital wallet for ArDrive.  If you’ve ever used MetaMask before, you’ll find Wander similar.

Wander simplifies the login experience because your wallet is encrypted and safely stored within Wander. ArDrive can then securely access it as needed.

* * *

## Get Started

1.  Go to the [**Wander website**](https://arconnect.io) to download the Wander Chrome extension

2.  Press on the ‘Download for Chrome’ button

3.  You will be redirected to the Wander page of the Chrome store

4.  Once at that page press the ‘Add to Chrome’ button

5.  A pop-up will appear and please press ‘add extension’ and you will be directed to the Welcome page for Wander

## Download Wander

We are now on the “Welcome to Wander” page

1.  Create a password for Wander. This password is not the same as your password for ArDrive.

**Note:** Make sure to write down your Wander password as it can’t be recovered.

2\. You will be asked to either create a new wallet or load a wallet.

## Add a Wallet

-   To make a new wallet with Wander, press the “New Wallet” button, then copy the seedphrase and download the keyfile.

**Note:** Make sure to save your seedphrase and keyfile somewhere safe as they are not able to be recreated if lost.

-   The new wallet will be loaded into Wander automatically, but will have no AR.

## Log into the ArDrive using the digital wallet from Wander

1.  Visit the [**ArDrive login screen**](https://app.ardrive.io)

2.  Select the Wander option

3.  Log in with your ArDrive password

---

# Attach A Drive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-attach-a-drive/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Attach a shared public or private Drive to your account from a Drive link.

Attach Drives is the way to receive a Public or Private Drive that has been shared with you. There is no cost to attach a Drive to your account or download any files from that Drive.

## Use a Drive Link to attach a Drive

The easiest way to attach a Drive to your account is to simply click on a Drive link that has been given to you. Drives are automatically attached if they are accessed via a Drive Sharing link.

Once you click on the link (and [login to ArDrive](/help), if needed) your new Drive will appear under a ‘Shared Drives’ heading in the left side menu.

## Use the new button to attach a Drive

If you have the 36-character Drive ID, you can also use the **+NEW** button to attach a Drive.

Go to the control center of ArDrive and click the **+NEW** button.

Four options will appear including ‘Attach Drive’.

Click on Attach Drive and a popup box will appear at the center of your screen.

The pop up will have a place for you to put your 36-character Drive ID that your friend shared with you and a box that allows you to name the Drive. You can choose any name you wish for this Drive. It doesn’t have to be the same name that the original creator used.

Once you press ‘attach’ the Drive will appear under a ‘Shared Drives’ heading in the left side menu.

---

# Create A Snapshot

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-a-snapshot/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Create Drive snapshots to speed up future syncing for large public or private Drives.

## Snapshots are rollups of your Drive - it makes syncing your Drive in future sessions much faster.

**QuickSync** a roll up of your Drive's ArFS metadata into a single snapshot, meaning much faster syncing times for your Drive in future sessions.

This feature greatly improves the experience of power users with large Drives, all thanks to the ArFS protocol. Snapshots can be created for both your public & private Drives and decreases the sync time needed when you share/attach Drives with snapshots.

1.  Select the Drive you want to create a snap shot for, hit the **New** button, and select **Advanced**, and **New Snapshot**.
2.  Select **proceed**.
3.  ArDrive determines the size & cost of uploading the Snapshot, click **upload** to proceed.
4.  Your Snapshot is created & the next time you login, you'll notice faster Drive syncing.

---

# Create An Arweave Manifest

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-an-arweave-manifest/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Create an Arweave Manifest from a public Drive or folder to give permanent files friendly paths.

# Overview

Arweave Manifests can unlock new and unique experiences that can only be made possible on the Permaweb.

An Arweave Manifest is a mapping of Arweave Transaction IDs and friendly names. This map is put into a standardized, JSON structure and permanently stored on Arweave, and it allows users to create logical groupings of content. Arweave Gateways can interpret this structure, so users can then reference individual transactions by their file name and/or path.

Manifests allow you to use friendlier URLs like...

[https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/0.png](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/0.png)
[https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq\_8g4oeiGP5RnA/333.png](https://turbo-gateway.com/Bgw5-GwpymUoe5VMeb-No9WWXpjWsq_8g4oeiGP5RnA/333.png)

...rather than opaque Arweave transaction IDs that ordinarily reference your data:
[https://turbo-gateway.com/ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0](https://eev46qzbxsuwzsou7p4xu3trfpa63x6b3hpon5l46hnrisudhswq.turbo-gateway.com/ISvPQyG8qWzJ1Pv5em5xK8Ht38HZ3ub1fPHbFEqDPK0)
[https://turbo-gateway.com/nHuFsR2Y-e\_qtnsmj0avn37CTWxlZ4zvG\_CKwtldK3I](https://turbo-gateway.com/nHuFsR2Y-e_qtnsmj0avn37CTWxlZ4zvG_CKwtldK3I)

With ArDrive, any Public Drive or Folder can be turned into a Manifest. The file names and paths would each be represented within the Arweave Manifest, making it easier for developers and users to reference their content by the names used on their local file systems.

Want to learn how it works and see some examples? [Check out our detailed technology article.](/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-arweave-manifests/)

# Instructions

Creating an Arweave Manifest is easy with ArDrive.

1.  Navigate to your Public Drive, and click the “New” button and "Advanced" and select “Create Manifest”.

1.  Enter in the name for your Arweave Manifest and click “Next”

1.  Select the location to create the Manifest out of.

    1.  Selecting the top level of the Drive will create a manifest out of all of the Drive’s content, including subfolders.

    2.  Selecting an individual folder will create a manifest out of all of that Folder’s content, including subfolders

2.  View and confirm the price of the Manifest and upload it to ArDrive

After the Manifest is created and uploaded, it will have a special icon indicating that it is an Arweave Manifest.

Congratulations! You can now access any of the Arweave Transactions referenced in the Manifest using the its friendlier file path and name. [See our technical article on for more references.](/help/understanding-the-permaweb-general-arweave-manifests/)

Clicking the “Preview Link” for a Manifest will load the default `index.html` page referenced within the Manifest.

Clicking the “Download Link” for a Manifest will download the default `index.html` page referenced within the Manifest.

No `index.html`? No problem! The first file contained in the Manifest will act as the default file that is loaded when the Manifest is previewed, and all other files can still be referenced with their friendly names.

**NOTE:** Check the paths that your Manifest is using by checking the raw endpoint: `turbo-gateway.com/raw/ManifestTXID` into your browser.

# Updating a Manifest

Need to make changes to your permanent web app or NFT collection? ArDrive makes it easy for users to quickly adjust a specific file or folder, and then recreate a new manifest containing the updated transaction IDs and friendly names. Just like any other file in ArDrive, a new Manifest revision can be created by reusing the existing Manifest name, allowing permanent version control over your site or app.

# Want it Custom?

The ArDrive CLI also allows you to create a manifest out of a given Public Drive or Folder, and takes it a step further.

With the CLI, developers can quickly create and export a preview of their manifest. This preview can be modified in a standard text editor to fit any use case, like adding other Arweave Transaction IDs that aren’t contained within your Drive, or changing the friendly names. Once the manifest has been customized, the CLI can quickly upload it, using the custom “Arweave Manifest” content type.

Detailed instructions can be found on our [Docs Portal](https://docs.ardrive.io).

---

# Create New Drive

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-new-drive/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Create a new public or private Drive for permanent file storage on the permaweb.

ArDrive is built around “Drives” - permanent digital storage containers. Drives are like hard drives on the permaweb where you can put all kinds of files - audio, video, photo, code - and group them in folders to your heart’s content.

You must create a Drive before you start uploading your files.

## Getting Started

To get started with ArDrive, you’ll first want to create a Drive.

Hit the **+NEW** button and a drop down list will appear with our seven options.

Select “New Drive”

A pop screen will appear that gives you the option to name your Drive and to select whether it is public or private.

★ If you select “Private” then only you will have access to that Drive. If you select “Public” you are giving access to all files in the Drive to everyone forever. An automatic URL will be created for this Drive and this URL cannot be deleted.

When you create a new Drive, the default is for it to be private. Be careful! If you accidentally publish your information to a public Drive there is no delete button.

## New Drives added to the Sidebar

Once you create the Drive it will now appear on the sidebar list under personal Drives.

After you select the Drive, you can now add folders or upload files to it.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="lurTntJbd1U" />

---

# New Folders

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-new-folders/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Create folders to organize files inside an ArDrive Drive.

After [creating a Drive](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-new-drive/), you can upload any file you want to it, but you can keep your Drive organized by creating folders.

Click on the **+NEW** button and choose “New Folder”.

Once you create a new folder it will appear under the Drive that you have assigned it to.

Select the folder that you have created, and it will take you another level down into that folder.

Navigate easily through your Drive by clicking on the folder names that now appear as breadcrumbs in the top navigation.

**NOTE:** Folders cannot be shared at this time.

---

# The New Button

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-the-new-button/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Use the +NEW button to create drives and folders, attach shared drives, upload files, and create manifests or snapshots.

The first thing to know about using ArDrive is that the big red **+NEW** button in the top left corner is your best friend.

The **+NEW** button is the control center of the app, where you control your Drive:

-   [Create New Drives](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-new-drive/)

-   [Create New Folders](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-new-folders/)

-   [Attach a Drive](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-attach-a-drive/) made by someone else

-   [Upload Files](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-uploading-files/)

-   [Create a manifest](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-an-arweave-manifest)
-   Create a Snapshot

---

# Uploading Files

Canonical URL: https://ardrive.io/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-uploading-files/
Category: Using ArDrive Apps
Section: Using ArDrive
Description: Upload files from a Drive using the +NEW button, review the one-time storage price, and wait for upload confirmation.

After [creating a Drive](/help/using-ardrive-apps-using-ardrive-create-new-drive/) you are ready to upload a file.

Click into the Drive where you want to upload your file.

Now click on the **+NEW** button and select “Upload file(s)”

ArDrive will now allow you to select from the file(s) on your desktop.

Select which file you want and press “Upload”

ArDrive will then calculate the cost of the file(s) you want to upload to the permaweb. A pop up screen will appear that will confirm the file(s) you have selected, the size of each file, and the one-time cost.

You will now have the ability to cancel the transaction at no cost or upload the file for the cost indicated.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="51Gpg3-GFZw" />

## What file sizes can I upload?

Upload limits depend on upload method, browser, and platform. See [ArDrive App Limits](/help/faqs-help-and-troubleshooting-guides-ardrive-app-limits) for the current limits.

Our calculation of file size may be slightly larger than the file size listed on your computer given the unique way that data is stored and encrypted on the permaweb.

## How long does it take to upload?

Decentralized, permanent data storage doesn’t happen in an instant. Upload speeds are dependent on your internet connection. ArDrive waits til there are 15 confirmations on your uploaded data to ensure there are no forks in the chain.

**NOTE:** Do not leave from the uploading screen until the upload completes. If you exit the browser before it has completed your file will not be uploaded. You will not be charged if a file does not fully upload, but any progress towards uploading your file will be lost.

## Understanding File Status

ArDrive tries to track the status of each transaction in order to let you know when your files are stored on the network.

-   Green status means the file was successfully replicated and mined on the network.

-   Orange status means the file upload transaction is in progress.

    -   It could take up to 30 minutes for this process to complete, permanence requires patience!

-   Red status means the file upload transaction may have failed

    -   If a file is not mined, the AR payment made for this file is refunded.
